mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and real GDP indicates synchronized demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases, or t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI increases above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains negative over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and supports corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of months, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with rising personal consumption expenditures signal robust household balance sheets and durable demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a tightening cycle indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.
